mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in retail sales signals near-term revenue growth for cyclical firms and supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days; negative momentum predicts the opposite.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available RSAFS was 718867.0 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if months with positive 3-month RSAFS momentum do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than months with negative momentum in walk-forward tests, or if the return spread is negative over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.5%, restrictive discount-rate pressure lowers equity valuation multiples and predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS was 4.64 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations with FEDFUNDS above 4.5% deliver positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases, or if mean triggered returns exceed the unconditional mean by more than 25 basis points.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate greater than 0.3 percentage points signals deteriorating labor demand and rising recession risk, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available UNRATE was 4.2 as of one month before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment-rise episodes have non-negative average 60-day S&P 500 returns in walk-forward evaluation, or if their hit rate for negative returns is below 50% across a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI while the federal funds rate is above 4% signals sticky inflation under restrictive policy, increasing the risk of delayed easing and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized growth rises versus prior 3 months and FEDFUNDS > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available CPIAUCSL was 316.441 and FEDFUNDS was 4.64
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition fails to produce lower 20-day S&P 500 returns than either high FEDFUNDS alone or accelerating CPI alone in out-of-sample tests, or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in at least 70% of rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Rising 3-month industrial production indicates stronger real output and operating leverage for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available INDPRO was 102.2805 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO growth is followed by lower 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative 3-month INDPRO growth in walk-forward tests, or if the Rank IC is below 0.03 over two consecutive 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A year-over-year decline in housing starts greater than 10% signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and lower construction-linked earnings, predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest available HOUST was 1311.0 as of two months before the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST contraction episodes show positive average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the negative-return hit rate is below 52% in walk-forward samples.

mechanism_narrative: When payroll growth slows while the unemployment rate rises, the labor market is transitioning from expansion to slack, reducing expected earnings growth and predicting negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month growth rate declines versus prior 3 months and UNRATE rises > 0.2pp over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest available PAYEMS was 159288.0 and UNRATE was 4.2
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-slowdown and unemployment-rise condition does not underperform unconditional 20-day S&P 500 returns by at least 50 basis points on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in the federal funds rate while unemployment remains below 4.5% signals insurance-style easing rather than recession easing, supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines over 3 months and UNRATE < 4.5%
evidence_refs: fact:latest available FEDFUNDS was 4.64 and UNRATE was 4.2
failure_condition: Reject if the triggered condition produces negative average 5-day S&P 500 returns, or if its average return is not higher than rate-decline episodes with UNRATE >= 4.5% in walk-forward evaluation.
